text.title: Agile Hybrid Approaches Examples
text.summary: Worked decision record for a regulated claims platform.

example.title: Regulated claims platform — mode decision
example.context: 8-FTE team, $200k/outage SLA fines, regulator change-log cadence quarterly, team Scrum maturity 4/5.
example.code:
## Dimensions (Boehm + Turner) - requirement_volatility = 0.4 (regulator change-log quarterly, low volatility) - team_skill_uniformity = 0.6 (skill-matrix Q2 — mostly senior, 2 mid-level) - failure_cost_low = 0.2 (SLA fines $200k per outage — high failure cost) - team_size_small = 0.5 (8 FTE delivery team) - culture_fit_agile = 0.7 (team Scrum maturity score 4/5) - domain_uncertainty_high = 0.5 (BA discovery notes Q1 — moderate)
avg = 0.483 → hybrid
## Named hybrid tilt - Predictive on regulatory milestones + audit gates: 90-day plan with named milestones; change-control board for scope. - Agile on UX + reporting layer: 2-week sprints, WIP limits, Kanban board for back-office work. - Boundary: any change touching the regulatory data model goes through change-control; UX changes flow through sprint planning.
## Re-evaluation triggers 1. Velocity volatility > 30% over 3 sprints. 2. Escalation rate > 1 per sprint for 2 sprints.
example.commentary: Average 0.48 lands in hybrid range; named tilt forces explicit per-workstream mode choice with boundaries; two measurable triggers ensure mode drift is detected and re-evaluated.
